mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects capacity constraints that erode profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO signals during tightening cycles yield positive 20-day forward returns in over 50% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL month-over-month increase above 0.6% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, triggering immediate negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.6% monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer spending expectations and generates positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and UNRATE signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of historical observations, or Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates persistent supply chain bottlenecks that suppress near-term equity valuations within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO signals during recovery phases produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances, invalidating the bottleneck transmission channel.
